title: Higher-moment target-stat features for binned numeric and categorical groups
summary: Augment simple target encodings with fold-safe mean, std, skew, median, min, max, and count statistics over categorical and discretized numeric groups.
rationale: The Kospintr baseline does not only target-encode category means; it constructs a family of target statistics for original categoricals and bucketized numerics. Higher moments can tell the model whether a group has stable pit risk, bimodal behavior, or sparse/unreliable history.
implementation_hint: Within each CV fold, discretize key numerics such as RaceProgress, TyreLife, LapTime_Delta, and Cumulative_Degradation, then compute smoothed group target mean/std/skew/median/min/max/count for selected categoricals and bins. Apply fold-training stats to validation/test with global fallback and compare against mean-only encodings.
expected_effect: May add reliability and shape information beyond plain target means, especially for sparse Race/Compound/Stint/TyreLife buckets.
risk: Large feature count and multiple target-derived statistics increase leakage/overfit risk; must be strictly fold-local with smoothing.
